title: Radiant Sin
author: Katee Robert
blurb: When Cassandra is trapped at a lavish Olympian party, the only escape is playing girlfriend to the golden, terrifyingly perfect Apollo — a role that turns very real, very fast. A scorching, glossy Olympus retelling of Cassandra and Apollo.

title: Cruel Seduction
author: Katee Robert
blurb: When Aphrodite arranges a marriage between her Adonis and his ex Psyche's sister Eurydice, everyone involved is playing a very dangerous game — until Hephaestus enters the room. A poly-adjacent, scorching Olympus retelling.

title: Midnight Ruin
author: Katee Robert
blurb: Eurydice is done being everyone's pawn — even when it means striking a bargain with Charon, the ferryman of the dead, and confronting the two men she thought she'd left behind. Katee Robert's most poly-forward Olympus book yet.

title: Shadow and Bone
author: Leigh Bardugo
blurb: In a nation split by a monstrous wall of darkness, orphan Alina Starkov discovers she wields a rare, world-changing light — and is whisked into the seductive Grisha court of the enigmatic Darkling. The book that launched the Grishaverse.

title: Siege and Storm
author: Leigh Bardugo
blurb: On the run with Mal, Alina meets the charismatic privateer prince Nikolai Lantsov — and the Darkling's power stretches even into hiding. The middle Grishaverse book: bigger world, sharper politics, more heart.

title: Ruin and Rising
author: Leigh Bardugo
blurb: With Ravka on the brink and the Darkling closing in, Alina must find the final amplifier — and decide what kind of Saint, and person, she really is. The Shadow and Bone trilogy's soaring, sacrificial finale.

title: Crooked Kingdom
author: Leigh Bardugo
blurb: Betrayed and cornered, Kaz and his crew of Dregs plan the con of their lives to save one of their own — while every gang, royal, and Grisha in Ketterdam hunts them. The masterful conclusion to the Six of Crows duology.

title: King of Scars
author: Leigh Bardugo
blurb: Nikolai Lantsov is a king with a monster inside him — and a country on the brink of another war. As Zoya, Nina, and old ghosts collide across Ravka, the Grishaverse tilts toward something ancient and terrible. Nikolai's first solo outing.

title: Rule of Wolves
author: Leigh Bardugo
blurb: Kingdoms move against Ravka, saints wake in the dark, and Nikolai must decide whether the demon inside him is a curse or a weapon. Bardugo weaves every Grishaverse thread — Alina's world, Ketterdam's crows, the ice court — into one blockbuster finale.

title: Glass Sword
author: Victoria Aveyard
blurb: Mare Barrow — the lightning girl who bled red and struck silver — leads the ragtag Scarlet Guard to find others like her before the Silver crown finds them first. Aveyard's world darkens and the body count climbs.

title: King's Cage
author: Victoria Aveyard
blurb: Locked in Maven's court and paraded as a broken toy, Mare must survive a king who is both her tormentor and someone she once tried to love. Cameron's chapters, an unraveling capital, and Mare's slow return to herself.

title: War Storm
author: Victoria Aveyard
blurb: Kingdoms tear at each other and lightning splits the sky as Mare, Cal, and the Scarlet Guard drive toward the throne — and Mare has to choose between the boy she loves and the future her people deserve. The Red Queen finale.

title: The Bear and the Nightingale
author: Katherine Arden
blurb: In a remote Russian village where fairy tales protect the household from the winter dark, wild Vasya can see and speak to the old spirits her people are being taught to fear. When a stranger arrives preaching a new god, Vasya must ally with a frost demon to save everything she loves. Lush, snowbound, and beloved.

title: The Girl in the Tower
author: Katherine Arden
blurb: Disguised as a boy and riding as a warrior, Vasya lands in medieval Moscow as bandit raids devastate the countryside — and the frost demon Morozko is nowhere to be found. Court intrigue, sibling love, and quiet magic in a Rus on the edge.

title: The Winter of the Witch
author: Katherine Arden
blurb: With Moscow burning behind her, Vasya walks into the deep dark of the fairy world to save her country, her family, and the frost demon she loves. Katherine Arden's Winternight trilogy ends with all the ache and glory you'd hope for.

title: The Wrath and the Dawn
author: Renée Ahdieh
blurb: Every dawn, the Caliph of Khorasan takes a new bride and executes her at sunrise. Shahrzad volunteers, planning to kill him herself for what he did to her best friend — until his stories, and his eyes, complicate everything. A lush 1001 Nights retelling.

title: The Rose and the Dagger
author: Renée Ahdieh
blurb: Torn from Khalid and hiding in the desert, Shahrzad must break the curse that's killing his kingdom before rival armies destroy them all. The blazing conclusion to the Wrath and the Dawn duology.

title: These Violent Delights
author: Chloe Gong
blurb: In 1920s Shanghai, Juliette Cai and Roma Montagov are heirs to warring gangs — and blood-feuding exes. When a supernatural madness starts killing on both sides of the city, they'll have to work together or watch Shanghai fall. Romeo and Juliet with venom and neon.

title: Our Violent Ends
author: Chloe Gong
blurb: Juliette has picked sides, Roma has picked survival, and Shanghai is picking up the bodies of everyone caught between. Gong follows Shakespeare with unflinching heart into the finale of the duology.

title: The Ten Thousand Doors of January
author: Alix E. Harrow
blurb: January Scaller lives as the ward of a wealthy collector — until she finds a book that describes a girl exactly like her walking through impossible doors between worlds. Alix E. Harrow's stunning, romantic debut about escape, portals, and reading yourself free.

title: The Once and Future Witches
author: Alix E. Harrow
blurb: In 1893 New Salem, three estranged Eastwood sisters cast a small spell that opens a very old door — and awakens the movement to bring witching back into the world. Suffragism, sisterhood, and quiet, aching magic.

title: Spinning Silver
author: Naomi Novik
blurb: Miryem, a moneylender's daughter, boasts she can turn silver to gold — and catches the ear of a fae king who wants to hold her to it. Three heroines, a Rumpelstiltskin skeleton, and Novik's signature snowbound worldbuilding. Standalone sister to Uprooted.

title: Legendborn
author: Tracy Deonn
blurb: Bree Matthews enrolls in an early-college program to escape the grief of her mother's death — and stumbles into a secret society of descendants of King Arthur's knights, one of whom carries something that belongs to her family. Southern Black magic, Arthurian legend, and a slow-burn romance.

title: Bloodmarked
author: Tracy Deonn
blurb: Now claimed by the Round Table — and marked by something older than any of them — Bree walks into the political nest of Arthurian bloodlines with a rebellion in her hands. Deonn digs deeper into ancestral magic, grief, and impossible loyalty.

title: Book of Night
author: Holly Black
blurb: In a world where a few people can manipulate shadow into power, Charlie Hall is a bartender, con artist, and reformed thief — until an old case surfaces and the boyfriend she thought was safe turns out to be much stranger. Holly Black's adult debut.

title: Slaying the Vampire Conqueror
author: Carissa Broadbent
blurb: Two centuries before Oraya, a warrior priestess named Iliana and her captor — a lethal vampire king from the far south — are locked together on a long march across occupied land. A gothic novella in the world of Serpent and the Wings of Night.

title: For the Throne
author: Hannah Whitten
blurb: Neve is trapped in the Shadowlands with the exiled Solmir — a rival, a maybe-monster, an ally she can't afford to want. As Red hunts for her sister above, the Wilderwood duology closes with a slow-burning, aching redemption.

title: A Dance with the Fae Prince
author: Elise Kova
blurb: Katria Applegate has spent her life as the plain-Jane of her family — until the coldly perfect Lord Fenwood buys her hand in marriage and takes her to a house full of secrets. Cozy, sweet, closed-door fae romance in the Married to Magic world.

title: A Duel with the Vampire Lord
author: Elise Kova
blurb: Once a year, the village hunter delivers the year's tribute to the mountain — and Floriane has been trained her whole life to kill the vampire lord who lives there. But he is not what her village told her, and the truth may cost her everything. Kova's spicier-tinged Beauty-and-the-Beast.

title: The City of Brass
author: S. A. Chakraborty
blurb: Nahri is a con artist working the streets of eighteenth-century Cairo — until a chance-spoken word summons a warrior of ancient djinn magic and drags her into the treacherous, gilded city of Daevabad. A lush, Middle-Eastern-inspired epic trilogy.

title: The Kingdom of Copper
author: S. A. Chakraborty
blurb: Five years into her exile inside a palace of enemies, Nahri plots her survival while Prince Ali runs from assassins and old wars stir along Daevabad's copper walls. The middle Daevabad book: darker, richer, sharper.

title: The Empire of Gold
author: S. A. Chakraborty
blurb: Cairo, again — but this time Nahri is not alone, Daevabad has fallen, and the war for the six djinn tribes is coming home to her. A finale that pays every promise the trilogy made.

title: Circe
author: Madeline Miller
blurb: Cast out of her father's halls for the crime of loving a mortal, the witch Circe carves out an immortal life on a lonely island — and confronts the gods, monsters, and mortals who wash up on her shore. Companion in spirit to The Song of Achilles.
